svg.title: InfiniBand BTH (Base Transport Header) field layout
svg.desc: Bit-level breakdown of the 12-byte IB BTH across three 32-bit words
svg.text: BTH — Base Transport Header (12 bytes / 96 bits)
Present in every IB packet; immediately follows LRH (or GRH)
Upper 3 bits = QP type (RC/UC/UD); lower 5 bits = operation type and fragmentation marker
Solicited Event — triggers a completion event notification on the receiver
MigReq — requests migration to the alternate path for connection failover
Number of padding bytes appended to align payload to a 4-byte boundary
Partition Key — used for subnet partitioning and traffic isolation
Fence — forces this operation to wait for all prior RDMA Read / Atomic ops to complete
Destination Queue Pair number (24 bits); source QPN is carried in DETH for UD
ACK Request — instructs the responder to send an acknowledgement for this packet
Packet Sequence Number (24 bits, modulo 2²⁴) — used for ordering and retransmission detection
